channel.title: Fifth Language Blog
channel.description: Updates, design decisions, and release announcements from the Fifth language project.

item.title: The Story of Fifth Language
item.description: A tale of semantic ambition, late-night commits, and the emergence of human-AI pair programming.

item.title: A New Release Pipeline for Fifth
item.description: This post describes Fifth's new GitHub Actions release pipeline that builds self-contained compiler binaries across Linux, macOS, and Windows for both .NET 8.0 and .NET 10.0, complete with checksums and smoke tests to ensure every download works out of the box.

item.title: Announcing Fifth: A New Language For Knowledge Graphs
item.description: Fifth is a new experimental language built on .NET, designed to make working with knowledge graphs feel natural and expressive. It removes much of the boilerplate and friction typically involved in graph-centric development, offering clean syntax and built-in support for graph structures and queries.

item.title: [DEPRECATED] Announcing Graph Assertion Blocks in Fifth
item.description: Graph Assertion Blocks have been removed from Fifth. This post is kept for historical reference only. The language now uses RDF/SPARQL literals and operators instead.
